pattern: Track the last lowercase position and the first uppercase position per letter; a letter qualifies when all its lowercase occurrences finish before its first uppercase occurrence.
hints: For each letter, record the index of its last lowercase occurrence and the index of its first uppercase occurrence.
A letter is special only if it has both cases present and the last lowercase index is strictly less than the first uppercase index.
Scan the string once to collect last_lower and first_upper maps, then count qualifying letters.

edge_inputs.description: uppercase appears after all lowercase — qualifies
same letter: lowercase then uppercase then lowercase again
interleaved: qualifies for b but not a
all 26 letters qualify: lowercase block then uppercase block

stress.description: random string of up to 200 letters
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    import string
    chars = string.ascii_letters
    w = ''.join(rng.choice(list(chars)) for _ in range(size))
    return [w]
